codeReview.summary: The implementation adds a clean grouped horizontal bar chart to product-line.html comparing opportunity and risk across all product lines. The code is well-structured, follows existing patterns (template-literal SVG generation matching renderAreaChart), includes input validation, uses viewBox for responsive sizing, and has Chinese labels throughout. The worker verified with both streetwear and jade datasets via agent-browser. The level mapping (高→90, 中高→70, 中→50, 低中→30, 低→30) is reasonable and handles the fallback default of 50 for unknown values. Colors (green #10b981 for opportunity, red #ef4444 for risk) are appropriate and consistent with the project's color scheme. The chart is placed at the top of the page before individual product line sections as specified. All 135 existing tests continue to pass.

issues.description: Product line names are interpolated directly into SVG text elements via template literals (innerHTML). While the data comes from trusted local JSON files (not user input or URL parameters), this is inconsistent with the AGENTS.md XSS convention which says 'use textContent for untrusted values'. Since the data source is the application's own JSON, this is acceptable but worth noting.

The AGENTS.md coding conventions state 'SVG charts: create elements programmatically via document.createElementNS', but this implementation uses template-literal string construction (returning HTML string with innerHTML assignment). However, this matches the existing pattern used by renderAreaChart (line 294) and all other render functions in components.js, so it's consistent with actual codebase practice rather than the stated convention.

The SVG text elements use 'alignment-baseline: middle' which has inconsistent browser support. 'dominant-baseline: central' is more reliable for cross-browser vertical centering of SVG text. However, since the worker verified rendering in browser and it appears correct, this is a minor robustness concern, not a blocking issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states 'SVG charts: create elements programmatically via document.createElementNS' but the actual codebase pattern (renderAreaChart, renderProductLineComparisonChart, and all other render functions) uses template-literal string construction with innerHTML. The convention doesn't match reality — every chart in components.js uses string templates, not createElementNS. AGENTS.md should be updated to reflect the actual pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: components.js line 294 (renderAreaChart) and line 431 (renderProductLineComparisonChart) both return template literal strings. No usage of document.createElementNS found anywhere in components.js (grep confirms 0 matches).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SKILL.md for dashboard-visual-worker has a typo in a CSS variable name: '--color-judgment-guancharzhong' should be '--color-judgment-guanchazu' (matching AGENTS.md). The skill and AGENTS.md are inconsistent on this pinyin spelling.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 3 lists 'var(--color-judgment-guancharzhong)' while AGENTS.md lists '--color-judgment-guanchazu'. The correct pinyin for 观察中 would be 'guanchazhong' but the actual CSS uses 'guanchazu' per AGENTS.md.

summary: PASS. The visual-product-line-comparison feature is correctly implemented. A grouped horizontal bar chart comparing opportunity and risk across all product lines is added to product-line.html using pure SVG with viewBox for responsive sizing. The chart includes Chinese labels, a legend distinguishing 机会 (green) vs 风险 (red), correct level-to-numeric mapping, and input validation for empty data. The implementation follows existing codebase patterns (template-literal SVG generation) and was verified with both streetwear and jade datasets. All 135 tests pass. Three non-blocking issues noted: innerHTML usage with trusted data (acceptable), template-literal SVG vs stated createElementNS convention (matches actual codebase practice), and alignment-baseline browser compatibility (verified working).
